nav.categoryLimit: Up to {max} categories at a time

forgotPassword.title: Reset your password
forgotPassword.description: Enter your email and we'll send you a reset link.
forgotPassword.sentDescription: If an account exists for that address, a password reset link is on its way.

updatePassword.title: Set a new password
updatePassword.description: Choose a new password for your account.

profile.title: Your profile
profile.aboutHint: Shown on your public profile page.
profile.aboutPlaceholder: Tell people what you work on and what your graphs are about.
profile.languageHint: Changes buttons and descriptions across the site. Your graphs' and posts' own content is never translated.

dashboard.title: My graphs
dashboard.empty: No graphs saved yet. Upload a vault to get started.
dashboard.confirmDelete: Delete this graph? This can't be undone.

home.tagline: Maximize the utility of your knowledge graphs -- and tap into everyone else's too.
home.browseExplore: Or browse Explore without an account →
home.betaNotice: Currently supports Obsidian vaults only. We're planning support for other graph-based note apps and knowledge tools too -- like Logseq, Roam Research, Tana, and RemNote.

vaultUploader.heading: Put your knowledge graph to work
vaultUploader.description: Upload your notes once, and Shreg turns them into a knowledge graph your own AI agent can query directly -- then explore and build on other people's shared graphs too. Parsing happens entirely in your browser, so nothing leaves your computer until you choose to save it below. Once it's saved, it's live as its own MCP endpoint, ready for any AI agent to connect to.
vaultUploader.noMarkdownFiles: No Markdown (.md) files found in that folder.

vault.heading: Your notes, connected
vault.body: A “vault” is just a folder of your notes, linked together like a personal wiki — tools like Obsidian make them easy to write. Upload one and Shreg turns it into a graph: the ideas inside, and how they connect.

analysis.heading: AI figures out what it's good for
analysis.body: An AI reads through the graph and writes a short description of what it's useful for, plus example questions it answers well — so anyone (including you) knows what to expect before relying on it.

mcp.heading: Any AI agent can query it live
mcp.body: MCP (Model Context Protocol) is a standard that lets AI assistants like Claude connect directly to outside data. Every graph gets its own MCP address — paste it into your agent's settings and it can search your notes while it answers you.

graphPage.evaluationRunning: Evaluation running — this takes about a minute. You can keep browsing or post while you wait.

graphSummary.noDescription: The author hasn't written a description for this graph yet.
graphSummary.mcpHint: Point your MCP client at this URL to query the full graph directly.
graphSummary.doesItHelp: Does it actually help? ({count, plural, one {# test question} other {# test questions}})
graphSummary.noEvaluation: The author hasn't run an evaluation on this graph yet.

evaluation.heading: Test as an MCP tool
evaluation.mcpHint: Point any MCP client at this URL to query this graph directly.
evaluation.noScenarios: Generate or write test questions above first, then run the evaluation.

graphAnalysis.heading: AI analysis
graphAnalysis.empty: No description or test questions yet. Generate them with AI, or write your own.
graphAnalysis.descriptionPlaceholder: What is this knowledge graph useful for? What kinds of questions does it help answer?
graphAnalysis.questionPlaceholder: Question a real user might ask
graphAnalysis.whyRelevantPlaceholder: Why does this graph give an edge answering it? (optional)
graphAnalysis.noQuestions: No questions yet — add one above.

versionHistory.noMarkdownFiles: No Markdown (.md) files found in that folder.

shareStructure.heading: Share full structure with the Knowledge Playground
shareStructure.description: Lets other users import this graph's actual nodes and connections into their own Playground sessions -- separate from the AI analysis and eval results, which are already visible to anyone once this graph is public. Turning this on also makes the graph public.

saveGraph.loginPrompt: to save and share this graph.
saveGraph.storageNotice: Saving stores this graph (including note contents) in our database.

nodeDetail.emptyState: Click a node to see its content and connections.
nodeDetail.unresolvedNote: This node is referenced by a [[wikilink]] but has no corresponding note in the vault.

graphCanvas.dragHint: Drag from one node's edge to another to link them

playground.heading: Knowledge Playground
playground.description: Import graphs, draw links between nodes from different graphs, and run analysis across all of them together. Nothing here is saved -- reloading starts fresh.
playground.noGraphsAvailable: No graphs available yet -- upload your own, or wait for others to share theirs.
playground.colorHintType: Colored by each node's own type (from its source graph's frontmatter).
playground.colorHintSource: Colored by which imported graph each node came from.
playground.colorHintClusterReady: Colored by cluster from the structural analysis below.
playground.colorHintClusterNotReady: Run structural analysis first to color by cluster.
playground.noStartEnd: Pick a start and end node first.
playground.noPathFound: No connection found between those nodes -- try adding a manual link.
playground.emptyField: Import a graph from the left to get started -- this field is empty until then.

feed.title: Explore
feed.emptyFiltered: No posts in these categories yet.
feed.emptyAll: No posts yet. Be the first — save a graph, generate a description, and share it to the feed.

follows.title: My Follows
follows.noPostsYet: No posts yet from people you follow.

friends.title: Friends
friends.noFriendsYet: No friends yet. Visit someone's profile to send a request.

posts.title: My Posts
posts.empty: You haven't posted anything yet. Share a graph from its page to get started.

postComposer.heading: Share to feed
postComposer.writePrompt: Write a post promoting this graph.
postComposer.willBePublic: Posting will also make this graph public.
postComposer.contentPlaceholder: What's this graph good for? Why should someone connect their agent to it?

completeProfile.heading: Finish setting up your account
completeProfile.description: Your account is signed in, but it's missing a username. Choose one to continue.

connect.title: Connect your graph to an AI agent
connect.intro: Every graph on Shreg is also a live MCP (Model Context Protocol) server -- a small set of tools an AI agent can call to search, read, and traverse that graph directly, instead of just guessing from its training data. Any client that speaks MCP can connect to it: Claude Code, Claude Desktop, ChatGPT, Cursor, Windsurf, VS Code, Gemini CLI, Cline, and more.
connect.knowing1: Each graph has its own endpoint URL -- one URL per graph, not one for your whole account.
connect.knowing2Prefix: Public graphs work with any client out of the box. Private graphs only respond to requests carrying
connect.knowing2Suffix: logged-in session -- since remote clients (Claude Desktop, ChatGPT, and most others) call this URL from their own servers, not your browser, they generally can't authenticate as you. If you want an agent to reach a graph, make it public first.
connect.knowing3: Connecting an agent doesn't change who can see the graph on Shreg -- it's a separate, additional way in.
connect.pickEndpoint: Pick which graph's endpoint to use below
connect.noGraphsMessage: Log in and upload a graph to get your own copy-pasteable endpoint -- for now, the instructions below use a placeholder URL you can swap your real one into.
connect.privateWarning: This graph is private -- most remote clients below won't be able to authenticate as you and will see it as not found. Make it public from the graph page if you want an agent to actually reach it.
connect.chooseWhere: Choose where you want to connect it
